pages.app.oldBrowserAlert.message: It looks like you're using an old browser that isn't supported. Some functionality may not work or may be broken. Please switch to a modern browser such as Firefox for full functionality.
pages.app.browser.title: RecipeSage - The Personal Recipe Keeper
pages.pricing.title: Pricing
pages.pricing.subtitle: Free and open source, no gotchas.
pages.pricing.intro.alwaysFree: RecipeSage is completely free for everyone. No ads, no time-limited trial, and no features locked behind a paywall.
pages.pricing.intro.openSource: RecipeSage is a hobby project I work on in my free time, alongside my other open source projects. The code is open source, so anyone can read it, fork it, or self-host their own copy.
pages.pricing.support.heading: Supporting the project
pages.pricing.support.body: If you'd like to support me, there's a donation option. It unlocks a few small bonus features as a thank you.
pages.pricing.support.hosting: Donations are entirely optional and go toward server and image-hosting costs.
pages.welcome.selfhostSubtitle: An unofficial selfhosted version of RecipeSage.com, a free recipe keeper, meal planner, and shopping list organizer for Web, iOS, and Android
pages.welcome.title: All of your recipes in one place
pages.welcome.subtitle: A free, open source recipe keeper, meal planner, and shopping list manager for Web, iOS, and Android. Works online or offline.
pages.welcome.description.1: RecipeSage is a personal cookbook and recipe organizer for cooks who collect recipes from everywhere: blogs, screenshots, PDFs, and handwritten family recipes. It gives them all one searchable home that travels with you between devices.
pages.welcome.description.2: Auto-import recipes from any website, photo, PDF, or text file. Sync your recipes, meal plans, and shopping lists across all of your devices, and load your recipes even when you're offline at the back of the grocery store. Share and collaborate with family and friends.
pages.welcome.description.features.impor: Save recipes from any link, photo, PDF, or document
Create recipes quickly and automatically from a web URL, a photo of a cookbook page or handwritten recipe card, a PDF, a Word document, or other text file
pages.welcome.description.features.nutri: Track calories, macros, vitamins, and minerals per serving, with auto-fill by pasting a nutrition label
pages.welcome.description.features.cooki: Ask the built-in cooking assistant for substitutions, techniques, conversions, and recipe ideas
pages.welcome.description.features.offli: Your recipe collection stays searchable even without internet, and syncs automatically across all of your devices
pages.welcome.description.features.mealP: Schedule your meals on a calendar, print them out, and share the plan with family
pages.welcome.description.features.shopp: Auto-categorized by grocery aisle and intelligently grouped: add recipes directly to a shopping list and similar items are combined
pages.welcome.description.features.scali: Scale recipes up or down and convert between metric and imperial units, including within instructions
pages.welcome.description.features.searc: Search every field within your recipes (including misspellings and similar words), tag recipes, and filter by label
pages.welcome.description.features.shari: Share recipes publicly or with friends, and collaborate on meal plans and shopping lists
pages.welcome.description.features.migra: Bring your collection from Pepperplate, Living Cookbook, Paprika, Mela, Crouton, Cookmate, Recipe Keeper, CopyMeThat, Evernote, Plan to Eat, MyRecipeBox, or any JSON-LD / CSV export
pages.welcome.description.features.expor: Back up your digital cookbook to PDF, JSON-LD, or text at any time: your data belongs to you
pages.welcome.description.features.brows: Browser extension for Firefox and Chrome
Clip recipes from any webpage in one click with the RecipeSage Clip Tool browser extension
pages.welcome.description.cta.noAds: Free and open source: no ads, no subscriptions, no tracking
pages.welcome.description.cta.crossPlatf: Works on desktop, iOS, and Android, online or offline
pages.welcome.description.cta.autoImport: Auto-import from any URL, photo, PDF, or text file
pages.welcome.description.cta.aiAssistan: Nutrition tracking, recipe scaling, and an AI cooking assistant
pages.welcome.description.cta.collaborat: Collaborate with family and friends on meals and shopping
pages.welcome.description.cta.selfHost: Self-host on your own server, or use the free hosted version at RecipeSage.com
pages.welcome.languages.title: In your language
pages.welcome.languages.description: RecipeSage is community-translated. The interface, ingredient parsing, and shopping list categorization all work in your language.
pages.welcome.demoVideo.label: RecipeSage auto-import demo

pages.legal.title: Legal, agreements and disclaimers
pages.about.title: About & Support
pages.about.metaDescription: Meet the developer behind RecipeSage and get help through Discord, GitHub, and the user guide.
pages.aboutDetails.title: More About Me & RecipeSage
pages.aboutDetails.1.title: A little about me
pages.aboutDetails.1.1: I'm a software developer in southern California. I do a lot of work on open source code and I spend a lot of time working on small projects.
pages.aboutDetails.1.2: RecipeSage is one of my hobbies, and I work on it in my free time alongside a lot of other projects. It's not my job and I don't make a living off of it, but it brings me a lot of joy to work on the project.
pages.aboutDetails.2.title: Why create RecipeSage?
pages.aboutDetails.2.1: I created RecipeSage to host recipes for me and my family. After trying multiple recipe programs online, I felt it would be best to create a recipe program that worked exactly as me, my mother, my girlfriend (now wife!), and my sibling wanted it to.
pages.aboutDetails.2.2: I'm a believer in open source software, and I want RecipeSage to be free to use. Everyone should be able to access and use this software, and I never want to subject RecipeSage users to ads or charge to be able to use the app.
pages.aboutDetails.2.3: If you'd like to contribute to support development, I'd really appreciate it. It's costly to run servers and host images. Contributing also unlocks some features for your account (features that are cost-prohibitive to offer for free) as a thank you.
pages.contact.title: Contact Me
pages.contact.subtitle: Contact Info
pages.contact.pleaseUserGuide: For any questions, please refer to the user guide:
pages.contact.discord: For any issues, bugs, or feature suggestions, you can reach out to me via our Discord:
pages.contact.github: While Discord is preferred, you can also report things directly on GitHub (slower response time):
pages.contact.cantUseDiscord: I can't use Discord and the documentation doesn't answer the issue/question I have
pages.contact.emailMe: I'm slow to respond to email (sorry!):
pages.contribute.title: Contribute
pages.contribute.subtitle: Contribute to RecipeSage
pages.contribute.mission: My goal is to keep RecipeSage open to anyone, without burdening the experience with ads.
pages.contribute.purpose: However, as RecipeSage grows, hosting costs for servers and images begin to add up.
pages.contribute.yearlyMonthly.descripti: You will unlock bonus features for the duration of your subscription
pages.contribute.oneTime.description: You will unlock bonus features for one year.
pages.contribute.bonus.title: As a thank you to those who make RecipeSage possible, your contributions will unlock some bonus features for your account. You will be able to:
pages.contribute.bonus.feature.2: Upload multiple images for each recipe
pages.contribute.bonus.feature.4: Higher daily usage allowance for recipe clipping, OCR/image & PDF recognition, URL & text-file imports, and nutrition autofill
pages.contribute.bonus.reason: Bonus features are accessible only to contributors because these particular features are costly for me to host.
pages.contribute.cta.1: I originally created RecipeSage for my own family to host recipes we enjoy sharing. Since then, RecipeSage has grown into a fully-fledged open source project.
pages.contribute.cta.2: Please consider contributing toward the ongoing maintenance of RecipeSage.
pages.contribute.minimum: Unfortunately the minimum amount is ${{amount}} due to transaction fees - apologies
pages.contribute.manage: To manage an existing subscription, click here
pages.contributeThankYou.title: Thank You!
pages.contributeThankYou.thanks: Thank you for being a part of the RecipeSage family and helping to keep it alive!
pages.contributeThankYou.bonus: Bonus features should be unlocked for your account shortly.
pages.contributeThankYou.questions: Please feel free to contact me with any questions, concerns or comments.
pages.contributeCancel.title: We need your help!
pages.contributeCancel.cta.1: RecipeSage is donation based - every contribution helps!
pages.contributeCancel.cta.2: Unfortunately, RecipeSage doesn't get many donations since it is a fairly small platform.
pages.contributeCancel.cta.3: In order to keep things free while paying for server costs, we need your help.
pages.contributeCancel.cta.4: Please reconsider - your contribution is truly appreciated!

pages.newLabelItemModal.label: Create a label
pages.newLabelItemModal.labelDescription: Labels are the primary organization method within RecipeSage. They can be attached to recipes to organize & filter them later. Some common label examples are "breakfast", "dessert", and "vegetarian".
pages.newLabelItemModal.groupDescription: Label categories are "categories for labels" and can be used to organize multiple different labels into groups. Some examples of common label categories are: "meal", or "diet". For example, you might put the labels "breakfast", "lunch", and "dinner" under a label category called "meal".

pages.assistant.welcome.2: The cooking assistant can respond to your cooking questions and help create recipes.
pages.assistant.welcome.3: Each message you send consumes one cooking credit from your daily allowance, which is shared with features like recipe clipping and imports. Contributors receive unlimited messaging.
pages.assistant.welcome.4: Only messages sent by you count toward your daily usage, not the assistant's responses.

pages.measurementConverter.variesNote: This ingredient's weight can vary; the value shown is a typical average.
pages.measurementConverter.ovenTable.foo: Celsius values are rounded from the exact Fahrenheit conversion; some charts round to the nearest 10°.
pages.cookingTemperatures.title: Cooking Temperatures
pages.cookingTemperatures.searchPlacehol: Search a food, doneness, or fry temp
pages.cookingTemperatures.safety.title: About these temperatures
pages.cookingTemperatures.safety.safeMin: Green chips mark USDA safe minimum internal temperatures. Measure with a food thermometer in the thickest part of the food, away from bone and fat.
pages.cookingTemperatures.safety.prefere: Grey chips mark personal preference values (doneness levels, BBQ pulls, oil temps). They are not food-safety recommendations.
pages.cookingTemperatures.safety.carryov: Carryover cooking: meat keeps cooking after coming off the heat, typically rising 5–10 °F / 3–6 °C. For large roasts, pull a bit below your target.
pages.cookingTemperatures.safety.source: Safe minimum temperatures from USDA FSIS and FoodSafety.gov.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.poultryD: Thighs and legs; cooks above the safe minimum for tender, collagen-rendered texture
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.stuffing: Cooked alone or inside the bird
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.beefWhol: Warm pink center. USDA safe minimum for whole beef cuts (steaks, chops, roasts, brisket flats).
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.porkWhol: Slightly pink center. USDA safe minimum for whole pork cuts (chops, roasts, tenderloin, fresh ham).
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.lambWhol: Slightly pink center. USDA safe minimum for whole lamb cuts (chops, racks, leg, roasts).
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.beefBlue: Very cool, deep red center; seared crust only
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.lambMedi: Mostly cooked through, hint of pink
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.salmonMe: Pink, just-translucent center; common chef target
Light pink center, fully cooked exterior
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.tunaRare: Red, just-warmed center; classic seared tuna
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.tunaMedi: Light pink center, fully cooked edges
Salmon, tuna, cod, tilapia, trout, halibut, snapper, bass
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.shrimpLo: Cook until the flesh is pearly and opaque.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.shellfis: Cook until the shells open during cooking.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.eggsFres: Cook until both the yolk and the white are firm.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.eggDishe: Quiches, frittatas, baked custards, casseroles with egg
Crème anglaise, pastry cream, lemon curd. Pull just before boiling; eggs scramble above ~85 °C / 185 °F.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.hamPreco: Reheating a cooked ham after storage
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.hotHoldi: Cooked food being kept warm (buffets, chafers, oven-hold). Hold at or above this temperature.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.dangerZo: Bacteria multiply rapidly in this range. Don't leave perishable food here for more than 2 hours (1 hour above 32 °C / 90 °F).
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarThr: Syrup drips in thin threads that dissolve quickly. Glazes, light syrups.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarSof: A drop in cold water forms a soft, pliable ball. Fudge, fondant, Italian buttercream.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarFir: Forms a firm ball that holds shape but flattens slightly. Caramels, soft marshmallow.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarHar: Forms a hard, sticky ball. Nougat, divinity, firmer marshmallow.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarSof: Sugar forms pliable threads that bend slightly. Taffy, butterscotch.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarHar: Brittle glass-like threads that snap cleanly. Brittle, toffee, lollipops, spun sugar.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.sugarCar: Sugar turns pale gold to deep amber. Light caramel at the lower end; bitter, almost-burnt at the top.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.chocolat: Melt: 50–55 °C / 120–130 °F • Cool: 28–29 °C / 82–84 °F • Re-warm back to the working range.
Melt: 40–45 °C / 105–115 °F • Cool: 27–28 °C / 81–82 °F • Re-warm back to the working range.
Melt: 40–45 °C / 105–115 °F • Cool: 26–27 °C / 79–81 °F • Re-warm back to the working range.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.pulledPo: Pork shoulder or butt; probe slides in with no resistance
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.brisketS: Probe-tender in the thickest part of the flat
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.beefBrai: Pot roast, chuck, brisket flat in liquid. Cook until fork-tender; meat shreds with light pressure.
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.porkRibs: Bend test or toothpick-tender between bones
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.fryGener: Most fried foods land in this window
pages.cookingTemperatures.items.fryFrenc: Second fry to crisp and brown

pages.import.jobs.subtitle: Imports continue to run even if you close the app once started
pages.import.jobCreated.message: Your data is being imported into your account now, and you can track it's status on the import page. You won't see anything in your account until the import job is complete.
pages.import.jobs.status.fail.emptyFile: The submitted import does not contain any recognizable recipes.
pages.import.jobs.status.fail.tooManyRec: Too many recipes for this import format. Please try again with a smaller batch.
pages.import.jobs.status.fail.unknown: An unknown error occurred. Please contact me.
pages.import.uploading: Uploading, please do not leave this page...
pages.import.description: RecipeSage supports importing a number of formats
pages.import.urls: Import from a list of URLs
pages.import.fileTooLarge: This file is too large to import into RecipeSage.
pages.import.fileTypeWarning: Warning! This filetype does not end with: {{ acceptableFiletypes }}. It may not work.
pages.importJsonLD.title: Import Recipes from JSON-LD File
pages.importJsonLD.description: This will import a JSON-LD file (ends in .json) file into your RecipeSage account. Please note that JSON-LD is a very specific file format, and not all .json files have the correct contents to be compatible with this import method. If you're having trouble with the import, please refer to the user guide. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importCookmate.title: Import Recipes from Cookmate File
pages.importCookmate.description: This will import a Cookmate (ends in .mcb) file into your RecipeSage account. If you're having trouble with the import, please refer to the user guide. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importRecipeKeeper.title: Import Recipes from Recipe Keeper Export
pages.importRecipeKeeper.description: This will import a RecipeKeeper database (ends in .zip) file into your RecipeSage account. Please note that not all .zip files are compatible, only those exported from RecipeKeeper. If you're having trouble with the import, please refer to the user guide. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importCopymethat.title: Import Recipes from CopyMeThat Export
pages.importCopymethat.description: This will import a CopyMeThat export (ends in .zip) into your RecipeSage account. Please note that you must export in HTML format from CopyMeThat to be supported by this importer. CopyMeThat's text export can be used with the "text files" RecipeSage importer if desired instead. If you're having trouble with the import, please refer to the user guide. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importLivingCookbook.title: Import Recipes from Living Cookbook
pages.importLivingCookbook.description: This will import a LivingCookbook file (ends in .lcb, .fdx, or .fdxz) file into your RecipeSage account. If you're having trouble with the import, consider re-exporting your database (in FDX or FDXZ format), or trying the import with the "Include stock recipes" option enabled. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importLivingCookbook.includeStock: Include stock "filler" LCB recipes (optional, not recommended)
pages.importLivingCookbook.includeTechni: Copy techniques to recipe notes (optional, not recommended)
pages.importPaprika.title: Import Recipes from Paprika File
pages.importPaprika.description: This will import a Paprika export file (ends in .paprikarecipes) file into your RecipeSage account. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importMela.title: Import Recipes from Mela File
pages.importMela.description: This will import a Mela export file (ends in .melarecipes, or a single .melarecipe) into your RecipeSage account. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importCrouton.title: Import Recipes from Crouton File
pages.importCrouton.description: This will import a Crouton export (a .zip archive containing .crumb recipe files) into your RecipeSage account. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importPepperplate.title: Import Recipes from Pepperplate
pages.importPepperplate.description: This will import all recipes from your Pepperplate account into your RecipeSage account. Please consider contributing if you have a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importPepperplate.incorrectCredent: It looks like the username or password you entered for Pepperplate is incorrect.
pages.importPepperplate.username.helperT: The email or username for your Pepperplate account
pages.importPepperplate.password.helperT: The password for your Pepperplate account
pages.importTextfiles.title: Import Recipes from Zip of Documents
pages.importTextfiles.description: This will import a set of documents compressed within a zip file (ends in .zip) into your RecipeSage account. Supported formats inside the zip: .txt, .rtf, .odt, .docx, .md, .markdown, .html, .htm, .org. Each document must contain a single recipe, and the zip may contain up to 100 documents (a mix of these formats is supported). Please refer to the user guide for how to create a file in the correct format to be imported here. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importEnex.title: Import Recipes from Evernote (enex) File
pages.importEnex.description: This will import recipes from an Evernote export (ends in .enex) into your RecipeSage account. Please refer to the user guide for how to create a file in the correct format to be imported here. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importUrls.title: Import Recipes from a list of URLs
pages.importUrls.description: This will attempt to import all of the URLs specified into your RecipeSage account using the autoclip functionality. Please keep in mind that some URLs may be unreachable or unparseable, and will not be imported. Please refer to the user guide for any issues. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importUrls.urls.helperText: Each URL must be on it's own separate line. Only URLs starting with http:// and https:// are supported.
pages.importCSV.title: Import Recipes from a CSV file
pages.importCSV.description: This will import all of the recipes contained in a CSV file into your RecipeSage account. Many programs output this format, but the internal structure can be somewhat arbitrary. Please refer to the user guide if you have any issues, otherwise contact me. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importPDFs.title: Import Recipes from PDF files
pages.importPDFs.description: This will import a set of PDF files compressed within a zip file (ends in .zip) into your RecipeSage account. Please refer to the user guide for how to create a file in the correct format to be imported here. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.
pages.importImages.title: Import Recipes from image files
pages.importImages.description: This will import a set of image files compressed within a zip file (ends in .zip) into your RecipeSage account. Please refer to the user guide for how to create a file in the correct format to be imported here. Please consider contributing if your file contains a large number of recipes or images.

pages.shoppingListIgnoreModal.title: Shopping List Item Ignore List
pages.shoppingListIgnoreModal.descriptio: Items listed here will be automatically excluded when adding a recipe to a shopping list. This is particularly handy for ingredients such as salt, which you likely don't need added every time you add an item to a shopping list.
pages.shoppingListIgnoreModal.instructio: Each entry should be on it's own line. Entries will match an ingredient if the ingredient contains any part of the line. If you enter "salt", it will match "1 tbsp sea salt finely ground".

pages.shoppingListIgnoreModal.placeholde: Keywords to ignore, each on their own line
pages.shoppingListCategoryOrderModal.des: Categories within this shopping list will be sorted in the order you list them here.
pages.shoppingListCategoryOrderModal.ins: Each entry should be on it's own line, and should match one of the predefined categories, or a custom category title you've entered when sorting items. Capitalization is ignored.
pages.shoppingListCategoryOrderModal.pla: Category titles, each on their own line

components.multiImageUpload.contributorH:
Hosting images is one of the biggest costs of running RecipeSage.

To keep the rest of the app free for everyone, uploading multiple images per recipe and storing images in high resolution are reserved for contributors as a thank you.

If you're enjoying RecipeSage, please consider contributing to help keep it alive!

services.quickTutorial.multipleRecipeSel: You're now in multiple recipe selection mode. You can click as many recipes as you'd like to select, then use the buttons that appear in the header to take bulk actions such as labelling or deleting. You can exit multiple recipe selection mode at any time by clicking the X in the header, or via the options menu.
services.quickTutorial.multipleLabelSele: You're now in multiple label selection mode. You can click as many labels as you'd like to select, then use the buttons that appear in the header to take bulk actions. You can exit multiple label selection mode at any time by clicking the X in the header, or via the options menu.
services.quickTutorial.splitPaneView.mes: Split pane view is only visible on devices with large screens (laptops, large tablets, etc). When split pane view is enabled, the side menu will always be visible. This feature is useful for optimizing the experience on larger devices.
services.quickTutorial.pinnedRecipes.mes: This recipe is now pinned! Pinning recipes provides a quick way to jump between multiple recipes quickly while cooking. Pinned recipes appear in the toolbar at the bottom of the screen. You'll see a bubble with the recipe image and the first letter of the recipe title. Pinned recipes will stay open until they are unpinned or until you close the application.
services.unsavedChanges.message: It looks like you have been editing something. If you leave before saving, your changes will be lost.
errors.offline: A connectivity error occurred. While offline, you're only able to do read-only actions, and view content if your device has it cached locally.
errors.unexpected: An unexpected error occurred and the app may not work correctly. Please reload the app and try again. If this continues to occur, please contact me.
errors.unexpected.selfhost: An unexpected error occurred. Please check your server logs as well as the self hosting FAQ.
errors.resourceNotFound.message: It looks like we tried to fetch something that doesn't exist. If this continues to occur unexpectedly, please contact me.
errors.creditsLimitReached.title: Sorry, limit reached
errors.creditsLimitReached:
The autoimport feature is particularly costly to host, so I've had to place a gentle limit on the number of times per day this feature can be used (sorry!). Your usage limit for automatic import & cooking assistant messages resets at 0:00GMT.

Contributing unlocks a larger daily allowance (10x the limit) since it helps to cover the costs. Sorry for the inconvenience!
webextension.action.tutorialIntro: The RecipeSage browser extension allows you to quickly snip recipes from the internet.
webextension.action.tutorialStep1: Navigate to a page that contains a recipe.
webextension.action.tutorialStep2: Activate the RecipeSage clip tool by clicking the RecipeSage icon in your browser's toolbar (you've already done this to view this popup).
webextension.action.tutorialStep3: The clip tool will automatically grab the contents of the page and find recipe content within it.
webextension.action.tutorialStep4: The saved recipe will open in a new tab once the process is complete.
webextension.action.openAfterImport: Open recipe in new tab after import
webextension.action.importingSubtitle: This may take up to 1 minute.
webextension.action.signInSuccessful: You are now logged in. Click the RecipeSage icon again to clip this website.
webextension.action.notLoggedInPrompt: Please login. It looks like you're logged out. Please close and re-open the clip tool to login.
webextension.creditLimitAlert:
Sorry, limit reached

The autoimport feature is particularly costly to host, so I've had to place a gentle limit on the number of times per day this feature can be used (sorry!). Your usage limit for automatic import & cooking assistant messages resets at 0:00GMT.

Contributing unlocks a larger daily allowance (10x the limit) since it helps to cover the costs. Sorry for the inconvenience!
webextension.action.clipFailed: Failed to clip recipe. If this continues, please report a bug.
webextension.action.missingTitle: Could not save recipe: no recipe title was detected on the page.
webextension.action.saveFailed: An error occurred while saving the recipe. Please try again.
webextension.action.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
webextension.action.duplicateConfirm: You already have a recipe saved from this page. Click OK to save another copy, or Cancel to skip.
webextension.settings.heading: RecipeSage Browser Extension Settings
webextension.settings.loggedOutMessage: You're not logged in. Click the RecipeSage icon next to the address bar to log in.
webextension.settings.loggedOutNote: Note: If you don't see the RecipeSage icon next to the address bar, you may need to enable it in your browser settings.
webextension.settings.serverHint: If you self-host RecipeSage, set the API and web URLs of your installation here. Leave a field blank to use the default. For a typical self-hosted install on yourdomain.com, the API URL is https://yourdomain.com/api and the Web URL is https://yourdomain.com.
webextension.settings.accountFetchError: There was an error while fetching your account data.
webextension.settings.invalidUrl: Must be a valid http(s) URL
webextension.inject.field.title: Title
webextension.inject.beforeUnload: You've made changes in the RecipeSage editor. If you leave before saving, your changes will be lost.
webextension.inject.pleaseLoginBody: It looks like you're logged out. Please click the RecipeSage icon to login again.
webextension.inject.saveFailedBody: An error occurred while saving the recipe. Please try again.
webextension.inject.duplicateConfirm: You already have a recipe saved from this page. Click OK to save another copy, or Cancel to skip.
